chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions on how to check the firmware version and begin the process of updating sensor configuration for Rheonics sensors using the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP).
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Updating sensor configuration

Modified on: Tue, 14 May, 2024 at 4:28 PM

Most Rheonics sensors support ability to update configuration of the sensor in field when the interface firmware version is 3.00 or higher.

1. To begin

Pre-requisites
Interface firmware version 3.00 or higher.
RCP version 5.2 or higher

Recommendation
Update sensor configuration over USB especially when you have updated the firmware and want to apply the previous configuration.

How to check the sensor firmware version?

You can use RCP to connect to the sensor over USB, or Ethernet (If you need instructions on how to do this, refer to the RCP connection article Running the RCP Program (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000297177)). Go to SERVICE tab (1) and click REFRESH (2). You will see the Interface FW version in the Software Info box (3). The screenshot below shows Interface FW version is 3.10 so it is higher than 3.00.

2. How to update the sensor configuration?

You should have received an updated configuration file(s) from Rheonics Support. Save these files to a folder that you can access from RCP.

Open RCP, connect to the sensor
key_facts: Most Rheonics sensors allow in-field configuration updates if the interface firmware version is 3.00 or higher.
A minimum RCP version of 5.2 is required for these updates.
It is recommended to update sensor configuration over USB, especially after a firmware update.
The sensor's firmware version can be checked in the 'Service' tab of the Rheonics Control Panel by clicking 'Refresh'.
Updated configuration files should be obtained from Rheonics Support and saved locally.
The screenshot demonstrates an Interface FW version of 3.10, which meets the 3.00 or higher prerequisite.

This needs a password which you should get from the RCP manual. You should read the manual to ensure that you are aware that Expert Mode should be used only by users who are trained and understand the consequences of making changes.
Once you are in EXPERT MODE, you will see the following screen:
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software version is 5.2.1.8.
A green status light indicates a successful sensor connection.
The sensor type should be correctly displayed as SRD.
Sensor and electronics serial numbers, along with interface and sensor firmware versions, are found in the 'Service' tab.
If system information is not populated, the 'Refresh' button should be used.
Accessing 'Expert Mode' requires a password, which can be found in the RCP manual.
Expert Mode is intended only for trained users who understand the potential consequences of making changes.
warnings_notes: The status light will be GREEN when the sensor is connected.
If sensor s/n and firmware versions are not populated, click REFRESH.
Expert Mode needs a password which you should get from the RCP manual.
You should read the manual to ensure that you are aware that Expert Mode should be used only by users who are trained and understand the consequences of making changes.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for uploading configuration files and performing a power reset on a Rheonics sensor using the Rheonics Control Panel software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel 5.2.1.8

Tabs: Measurement, Events, Settings, Service, Communication, Scaling, Calculation, Logger, Help

Operator Section:
Name / ID: Rheonics
Client: Rheonics GmbH

Communication Section:
Mode: USB (selected), Ethernet
Port: COM9
Autodetect (button)
IP: 10.5.33.101
Port: 10001
Apply (button)
Device Manager (button)
Status: (Green indicator)

Sensor Configuration Section:
Sensor Type: SRD
Import (button)
Export (button)
Disable Expert Mode (button)
Factory Reset (button)
Power Reset (button)

Units Section:
Viscosity: mPa.s (selected), cP, cSt
Density: g/cc (selected), kg/m³, lb/gal, lb/ft³
Temperature: °C (selected), °K, °F
Pressure: bar (selected), kPa, MPa, psi, torr, atm

Resolution Section:
Viscosity: 0.000
Density: 0.0000
Temperature: 0.0000
Pressure: 0.00

Instructions:
Click on IMPORT to import the config files sent by Rheonics Support.
Browse and select the file that should be uploaded to the Sensor.
Upload all the configuration files you recieved in sequence. It is advised to wait till the sensor LED blinks GREEN before uploading the next one.
Once all the files are uploaded, click POWER RESET.
This resets the power to the sensor and reboots the electronics.
key_facts: Configuration files for the Rheonics sensor are imported via the 'IMPORT' button in the Rheonics Control Panel.
Configuration files are typically provided by Rheonics Support.
Multiple configuration files should be uploaded sequentially.
After all configuration files are uploaded, a 'POWER RESET' is required to apply changes and reboot the sensor electronics.
A Power Reset resets the power to the sensor and reboots its electronics.
warnings_notes: Only configuration files for that specific sensor (or sensor electronics) can be uploaded.
If you get an error during upload, check if you are uploading the right file or contact support to get the correct file.
It is advised to wait until the sensor LED blinks GREEN before uploading the next configuration file in a sequence.

3. Troubleshooting
Issue
Reason
Solution
Error uploading the configuration file
Sensor may not be connected
Check correct COM port is selected.
Sensor status light will be GREEN
when sensor is connected.
Error uploading the configuration file
Incorrect sensor
Check you are uploading the
configuration to the correct sensor
serial number. All configurations are
valid only for the specified sensor
serial number.
What to do if your fw version is not 3.00 or higher?
Contact support.rheonics.com and request an update of the firmware.

specifications.pressure_units: bar, kPa, MPa, psi, torr, atm

key_facts: To check if a configuration update was successful, click EXPORT and email the file to Rheonics support.
The EXPERT MODE can be exited by clicking the 'Disable Expert Mode' button.
If there is an error uploading a configuration file, it may be due to the sensor not being connected or an incorrect sensor being selected.
For firmware versions below 3.00, users should contact support.rheonics.com to request a firmware update.
diagram_labels: What to do if your fw version is not 3.00 or higher?
warnings_notes: Sensor status light will be GREEN when sensor is connected.
All configurations are valid only for the specified sensor serial number.

chunks.page_summary: This page addresses the ability to enable/disable communication options in the field and provides a list of related Rheonics product and resource references.
product_names: HPHT Density and Viscosity meter, DVM
HPHT Density and Viscosity probe, DVP
chunks.extracted_text:
Can you enable/disable communication options in field?
Yes, you can. Request configuration codes by contacting support. There could be additional charge for the communication options and for the configuration.

4. References
Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) Manual (https://rheonics.com/resources/manuals/)
Electronics and communication options (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/)
Inline Viscometer, SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/)
Inline Density Meter, SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)
HPHT Density and Viscosity meter, DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/)
HPHT Density and Viscosity probe, DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/)
key_facts: Communication options can be enabled/disabled in the field.
Configuration codes are required and can be requested from support.

The page provides references to various Rheonics products and manuals, including the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) Manual, Inline Viscometer SRV, Inline Density Meter SRD, HPHT Density and Viscosity meter DVM, and HPHT Density and Viscosity probe DVP.
warnings_notes: There could be additional charge for the communication options and for the configuration.
